sections.biography-source-undefined:
Sun Feng was from Fuchun county, Wu commandery. He was the son of Sun Shao and thus a grandson of Sun Ce. He inherited Shangyu from Sun Shao and became its marquis.

He was executed in 270 by Sun Hao amidst rumors he would ascend the throne when Sun Hao was perceived dead by many for not appearing publicly for several months.

full_text:
Sun Feng 孫奉
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sun Feng (孫奉). Sun Hao saw him as a threat and made up crimes against him in order to execute him. Sun Feng was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Sun Feng lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Sun Feng was from Fuchun county, Wu commandery. He was the son of Sun Shao and thus a grandson of Sun Ce. He inherited Shangyu from Sun Shao and became its marquis.
He was executed in 270 by Sun Hao amidst rumors he would ascend the throne when Sun Hao was perceived dead by many for not appearing publicly for several months.
